description: Home network VLAN segmentation strategy and port assignment policy for lex's homelab. Use this when diagnosing network issues, planning firewall rules, or assessing device trust levels.

hardware.notes: WSL2 IP is in 172.21.0.0/20 subnet. Kubernetes pod network is 10.244.0.0/16. Router gateway is on LAN segment.

VLAN_10.name: Trusted
VLAN_10.purpose: Personal computers, dev machines, WSL2, homelab servers

VLAN_20.name: IoT
VLAN_20.purpose: Smart home devices with no trust — cameras, TVs, Google Nest, sensors

VLAN_30.name: Guest
VLAN_30.purpose: Untrusted visitors — internet only, no LAN access

VLAN_40.name: Infra
VLAN_40.purpose: Homelab infrastructure — Kubernetes nodes, Ollama server, NAS, switches

port_policy.description: TCP/UDP port assignments and firewall rules by trust zone. Applies to all VLAN-to-VLAN and VLAN-to-WAN traffic.

0-1023.name: Well-Known / System Ports
0-1023.policy: Restricted. Only explicitly allowed services open per VLAN. Never expose to Guest or IoT inbound.

notable_ports.53: DNS — all VLANs outbound to router only
notable_ports.80: HTTP — all VLANs outbound to WAN
notable_ports.443: HTTPS — all VLANs outbound to WAN
notable_ports.67-68: DHCP — all VLANs to router only

1024-49151.name: Registered / Application Ports
1024-49151.policy: Service-specific rules. Homelab services live here. IoT and Guest blocked from inbound access.

notable_ports.6080: noVNC viewer — browser-worker pod (Trusted only, never WAN)
notable_ports.11434: Ollama LLM inference (Infra only, never WAN)

49152-65535.name: Ephemeral / Dynamic Ports
49152-65535.policy: Client-initiated outbound connections. Allow outbound from all VLANs. Block inbound on all VLANs except established sessions.
49152-65535.notes: Used by OS for outbound TCP/UDP connections. No services should bind here permanently.

reserved_do_not_touch.description: Ports reserved for Xbox Live and gaming. Do NOT add firewall rules, NAT, or QoS policies affecting these without explicit approval.

xbox_live_ports.notes: Xbox Live requires these for NAT traversal and multiplayer. ER605 should have these open outbound on VLAN_10. Do not block, reroute, or rate-limit.

firewall_rules_intent: IoT → LAN: BLOCK ALL (IoT devices must never initiate connections to Trusted or Infra)
IoT → WAN: ALLOW (cameras, TVs need cloud access)
Guest → WAN: ALLOW (rate limited to 25 Mbps)
Trusted → IoT: ALLOW established/related only (Trusted can push to IoT, not vice versa)
Any → Ollama (11434): BLOCK from WAN, ALLOW from Trusted + Infra only
Any → k8s API (6443): BLOCK from WAN, ALLOW from Trusted + Infra only
Any → noVNC (6080): BLOCK from WAN and IoT, ALLOW Trusted only

dns_strategy.all_vlans: Point to router (ER605) for DNS. Router forwards to 1.1.1.1 or local Pi-hole.
dns_strategy.iot_vlan: DNS filtering recommended — block known IoT tracking domains
dns_strategy.notes: Do not expose internal DNS records to Guest VLAN

monitoring_hooks.syslog_target: Send ER605 syslog to WSL2 IP (172.21.x.x) port 514 — monitor_agent tails this
monitoring_hooks.snmp_community: Set read-only SNMP on ER605, poll from Infra VLAN only
it_agent_checks: alert if IoT device initiates connection to Trusted VLAN
alert if any VLAN device attempts to reach Ollama port 11434 from outside Trusted/Infra
alert if noVNC port 6080 is reachable from WAN
alert if Xbox ports (3074, 88) become blocked
